search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae conjunctivitis eye pain red flags PDF
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Unopened summaries were not used as evidence.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for corneal abrasion, keratitis warning features, and selected painful-red-eye triage; not exact for every ocular-pain cause.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA telehealth triage and UK first-contact eye guidance; in-person corneal, pressure, and fundal assessment remains outside telehealth scope.
UAE_applicability: DHA red-eye guidance is directly applicable in Dubai; international eye guidance requires UAE clinician review and local referral pathways.
recency_verification: Current DHA, College corneal-abrasion Version 15, microbial-keratitis Version 14, and official referral annex were opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: No newer official versions were identified on the opened pages.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped reduced vision, photophobia, fixed pupil, headache with nausea, trauma, chemical, and severe foreign-body warning fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped vision, trauma, contact-lens, photophobia, discharge, conjunctival, corneal, pupil, and acuity documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped return, referral, and clinician-arranged follow-up documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped corneal-abrasion, injury, foreign-body, pain, blurred vision, photophobia, watering, redness, and contact-lens fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports minor-injury, foreign-body and contact-lens context plus pain, blepharospasm, blurred vision, photophobia, watering, and redness.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped fluorescein assessment and referral documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician assessment of the involved area, fluorescein documentation, and referral when injury is deeper, contaminated, or possibly infected; medicine details are not mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped keratitis, acute pain, redness, photophobia, discharge, blurred vision, contact-lens use, and corneal opacity fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports usually unilateral acute redness, moderate or severe pain, photophobia, watering, discharge, blurred vision, corneal opacity or ulcer, and contact-lens, injury, surgery, exposure, dry-eye, or immune-risk context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped referral documentation for sight-threatening keratitis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Identifies microbial keratitis as sight-threatening and supports urgent ophthalmology documentation; treatment specifics are not mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped chemical-exposure and traumatic-eye warning fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists severe chemical burns, penetrating injuries, hyphema, embedded corneal foreign body, corneal or lid laceration, blunt trauma, severe abrasion, infective keratitis, and other urgent anterior-eye presentations.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped severe eye pain with nausea or vomiting, sudden pain, visual change, and pupil warning documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists sudden visual loss, sudden vision change, flashes or floaters with retinal signs, retinal detachment, acute diplopia, severe eye pain with nausea or vomiting, pain on eye movement, and new pupil defects.

unresolved_source_gaps: Broad keratitis diagnosis, eye-movement and intraocular-pressure examination, external and eyelid examination, red reflex, fundoscopy, visual fields, prior records, and exact medicine choices remain unsupported.
No diagnosis or emergency conclusion may be inferred from symptoms alone.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
